title: neurolepis elata
intro: Neurolepis elata, also known as the "Tasmanian peppermint bush," is a native Australian plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family, commonly referred to as citrus plants. The leaves of the Neurolepis elata plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Neurolepis elata include relief from digestive issues, colds, and flu, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
